page: Amazon Web Services m6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations align with average mid-tier expectations. However, memory bandwidth tests reveal a bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write operations scoring in the lower percentile. For machine learning workloads, the instance shows strong text generation speeds for small 135M models but exhibits poor prompt processing performance on larger 70B models.

The instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads. Given the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the absence of bundled local storage, it is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring steady CPU allocation without extreme memory bandwidth demands. Appropriate workloads include web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale text processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and efficient small-model text generation, balanced by average multi-core and database performance. However, uncached large-block memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for general-purpose applications, software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations that do not require high-speed local storage or extreme memory throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
